parsed_review.summary: CP00-844 is a descriptor-only RP27 Platform Extensibility review-evidence closeout bridge (risk C, 77 units, RP27.P09.M06.S14-RP27.P09.M10.S08). Static read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob; no commands executed) supports the pack-level production_ready (descriptor-verified) claim: (1) registry.js CP844 binding/requirements/case-set/Hermes packet/Claude packet/closeout handoff/descriptor/contract projection are runtime-closed and route to CP00-845/RP28.P00.M00.S01; (2) validators.js enforces coverage (deliverable {impl 49, claude_review 11, security_audit 8, test 4, ui 4, hermes_evidence 1}, phase/micro/title distributions, first/last unit, RP27-only, no duplicate IDs) plus per-row descriptor_only/no-secret/no-real-data/no-write guards, authority boundary (claude_is_final_approval=false, human approval required for runtime opening, local validation does not claim enterprise trust), Hermes emits_runtime_receipt=false, and handoff routing; (3) model.test.js asserts coverage/descriptor/projection against the live closeout-pack-plan and the committed contract artifact; (4) the committed contract lists CP00-844 as latest_pack/latest_projection with mandatory_artifacts, required_capabilities, safety_gates, no_leak_guards, and an all-false no_write_attestation, and a secret/real-data grep found nothing. Claude remains a read-only reviewer and not final approver; Hermes (H27) is confined to deterministic review packet/receipt evidence, not product runtime (hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). production_ready/runtime_ready are still false; the production_ready_flag is descriptor-verification only with enterprise trust deferred to human approval. command-evidence records node --check and rp27 platform validate/test at exit 0 [agent-reported, not re-executed in this read-only review]; the full closeout validation suite and the production_ready flip remain correctly gated post-review (gate_outcome=pending_valid_review_and_full_validation) and must complete with git diff --check clean before commit. No P0/P1/P2 issues; one P3 hygiene note that does not affect runtime-closed, no-write, no-real-data, or authority-boundary guarantees.

findings.title: Stale to_pack_id in shared ROW_EXTRAS.closeout_handoff template (inert for CP844)
findings.details: ROW_EXTRAS.closeout_handoff hardcodes to_pack_id 'CP00-821' and next_subphase_id 'RP27.P01.M05.S12'. createRow merges ROW_EXTRAS[key] into any case-set row whose key resolves to 'closeout_handoff'. CP844 section rows are drawn from CLAUDE_REVIEW_ROWS (slices), which contains 'Closeout criteria' but no 'Closeout handoff' row, so this stale value is never applied to any CP844 row. The CP844 descriptor-level closeout_handoff (createPlatformExtensibilityCp844CloseoutHandoff) correctly derives to_pack_id/next_subphase_id from PLATFORM_EXTENSIBILITY_CP844_PACK_BINDING (CP00-845 / RP28.P00.M00.S01), and validators check descriptor_only/no-secret/no-write/no-real-data per row rather than row-level to_pack_id. No functional impact on CP00-844.
findings.recommendation: Optionally derive ROW_EXTRAS.closeout_handoff.to_pack_id/next_subphase_id from the active pack binding (or remove the static values) to prevent latent drift for packs whose row sets do include a 'Closeout handoff' row. Not required for CP00-844 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking hygiene; out of CP844 functional scope; does not affect production_ready, no-write, no-real-data, or authority-boundary guarantees.
